headline: Mizuho says ignore Apple noise, flags key catalyst for Micron and memory in H2
summary: The first bear catalyst is a report that Apple is seeking U.S. government approval to source DRAM from Chinese chipmaker CXMT, which appears on the Commerce Department's Entity List. Apple's interest in Chinese memory is a symptom of industry-wide scarcity, not a Micron-specific problem.

content.title: Mizuho says ignore Apple noise, flags key catalyst for Micron and memory in H2
content.summary: The first bear catalyst is a report that Apple is seeking U.S. government approval to source DRAM from Chinese chipmaker CXMT, which appears on the Commerce Department's Entity List. Apple's interest in Chinese memory is a symptom of industry-wide scarcity, not a Micron-specific problem.

headline: 'One-time opportunity': South Korea bets big on AI boom
summary: Huge demand for the components that power artificial intelligence presents South Korea with an opportunity to bolster its chip industry against rivals such as China, analysts say.With China in particular racing to develop its tech industry, Seoul sees the boom period as a "one-time opportunity" to close the gap, said Lian Jye Su, a chief analyst at Omdia.

content.title: 'One-time opportunity': South Korea bets big on AI boom

headline: Micron Stock: Let's Talk About Those Long-Term Contracts
summary: Micron (MU) just reported blockbuster Q3 earnings. Revenue surged 4x year-over-year, earnings per share jumped 15x, and gross margins hit a massive 84.6%. The reasons are straightforward: an unprecedented AI buildout has driven explosive demand and pricing for high-bandwidth memory that sits alongside AI accelerators from the likes of Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD), with the supply crunch spilling into the broader memory market, too.

headline: The Real Price Of Intel Stock Is Three Years Away
summary: At a glance, Intel (INTC) stock looks expensive. Trading around $128.32, the shares command a multiple of about 118x this year’s expected earnings. headline: The Margin Squeeze Threatening Intel Stock
summary: If you hold Intel (INTC) stock, you’ve had a strong year. The shares have posted a 478% gain over the last 12 months, and the narrative has shifted in the company’s favor. Management now sees “clear signs that the CPU is reinserting itself as the indispensable foundation of the AI era.” The market has bought into this comeback story, with the stock trading near 91% of its 52-week high.
